model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Add mild regularization after the capacity and optimizer changes. Label smoothing should reduce overconfidence on CIFAR-10, and a small dropout rate can improve validation loss without changing the training contract.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last regularized run underperformed, so this step removes that bias and spends the remaining budget on a larger convolutional stack. The slightly lower learning rate is a conservative adjustment for the deeper model.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)
    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)
    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.1, total_iters=warmup_steps)
    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])","anchor":"","old":"","new":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Keep the current model and optimizer, but add a proper warmup phase before cosine decay. This is the safest recovery from the failed edit and should help stabilize early training under the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Undo the recent over-capacity and under-regularized head change, then trade batch count for more data per update under the fixed-step budget. That keeps the proven backbone intact while testing a higher-throughput training regime.","secondary_modes":["layout","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last gain came from increasing batch size, so the next low-complexity move is to push that axis further. This should expose more data per update under the fixed-step budget while keeping the proven model and optimizer settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Keep the successful large-batch setup and make the optimizer slightly more aggressive for the remaining short horizon. A higher learning rate with a faster-adapting second moment is the cleanest next attempt to push below the current validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch-size increase and lower beta2 improved convergence, but the run still appears slightly conservative. A small learning-rate bump is the lowest-complexity way to try to convert that remaining budget into a lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The run is now close to its floor, and the earlier gains came from reducing regularization pressure rather than adding more of it. Backing off AdamW decay a bit is the cleanest remaining test for whether the model is still slightly underfit.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current setup is close to saturation, and the earlier learning-rate increases still moved the loss downward. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher learning rate overshot the previous optimum, so revert to the proven step size and relax weight decay a bit further. {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.985)","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last reduction in weight decay slightly hurt, so restore the better regularization level and make Adam a bit more responsive. That keeps the stable large-batch regime intact while testing whether the optimizer still has room to tighten the loss.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.985)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The lower beta2 did not help, so restore the better optimizer setting and give the classifier more capacity now that the batch regime is much larger. This is the cleanest next test for whether the current model is still head-limited.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 512","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous head-width increase helped, so the next low-cost capacity test is to widen the classifier again while keeping the proven optimizer and batch settings fixed. This targets the remaining bottleneck without changing the convolutional trunk or training schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 512","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The 512-wide head was too large, so back off to an intermediate classifier width instead of reverting all the way. That keeps the stronger backbone and optimizer settings while probing the likely sweet spot between the last good head size and the overwide one.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Restore the classifier width from the last configuration that reached the current best loss. The wider head regressed, so this is the simplest way to return to the stronger capacity point without disturbing the proven optimizer and batch setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger-batch trend has been the strongest signal in the run, and 320 is a conservative step above the proven 256 setting. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size increases have been the strongest signal in the run, and 384 is a straightforward continuation of that trend. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the dominant improvement path throughout the run, and 448 is a controlled step beyond the current 384 setting.
